_meta.titus_model: 350R / 350F / 350R-SS (fixed-bar grille, no adjustable deflection blades) - IDENTICAL data source to RG-2, not the tag's originally-assigned 23R model
_meta.23R_rejected_2026-07-24: EG-2 was originally scoped to Titus 23R (aeroblade grille family, per the GRD schedule tag table). Pulled and precisely extracted the full 32-tier 23R breakpoint table (word-position PDF extraction, cross-checked, monotonic after sort) before building anything. Found 23R is from the same aeroblade/adjustable-double-deflection product family as 272RL (the cutsheet RG-2 moved AWAY from for the same reason) - at the same nominal size and NC<15 target, 23R produces roughly HALF the CFM capacity of 350R (e.g. 24x24/48x12 tier: 23R=750cfm vs 350R=1500cfm). Confirmed via raw NC comparison: 23R's NC at 400fpm on the 24x24 tier is 32, vs 350R's NC of 11 at the same velocity - 23R is inherently much louder despite having no explicit deflection-correction table of its own (its table header already states "45 DEFLECTION" as the tested condition, so no correction was applied on top of these raw values). User's decision: switch EG-2 to 350R/355R, the SAME data source as RG-2, since exhaust and return grilles are the same physical product type. The 23R extraction work is not reused - if 23R needs revisiting later (e.g. a different NC target), the raw group data is preserved in this session's transcript, not duplicated here.
_meta.no_deflection_correction_needed: Same as RG-2: 350R is a fixed-blade grille, no deflection-correction table or note, NC values used directly.
_meta.dash_convention: NC < 10 (standard Titus convention, consistent with every other cutsheet on this project, including RG-2's own 350R build)
_meta.target_nc: NC < 15, matching RG-2 exactly (user's explicit instruction: build EG-2 the same as RG-2)
_meta.size_ceiling: Capped at the '24x24'/'48x12' nominal size tier (max 1500 cfm at NC<15), same cap as RG-2 and the supply sidewall family.
_meta.architecture: Regular/Thin/Custom system, identical mechanism to RG-2 and the Supply Sidewall family: Auto Width/Auto Height carry the 32-tier cascade (built programmatically in C#, not hand-typed); Duct Width/Duct Height fall back through Custom Width/Custom Height (if Custom Size type and an override >0 is set) to Auto Width/Auto Height otherwise. Thin Profile (type Yes/No) selects the thin cascade branch. Ratio caps (4:1 thin, 2:1 regular) are already baked into the regular/thin picks below, reused verbatim from RG-2 - see RG-2_350R_sidewall_return.json for the full derivation/collapse reasoning per tier.
_meta.verified: 38 boundary test cases (regenerate-and-read), expected values computed programmatically from the same breakpoint array used to build the formula. 35 cases covering every breakpoint boundary on both Regular and Thin plus the over-capacity Sizing Warning trigger, plus 3 additional cases confirming the Custom type's fallback-to-Auto and override behavior. All 38 pass.
_meta.pre_rebuild_state: 9 legacy Types (6x6, 24x12, 14x8, 12x6, 24x24, 16x10, 12x12, 10x6, 10x4) with Duct Width/Duct Height as TYPE params - identical pre-rebuild structure to what RG-2 and the Supply Sidewall family had before their own rebuilds. 8 bogus types not present in any real Titus data (10x4, 14x8, 16x10, plus 6x6/24x12/24x24/12x6/10x6 replaced by the new Regular/Thin/Custom types) were deleted; one existing type (12x12) was renamed to 'Regular' and used as the basis to create 'Thin' and 'Custom'.

breakpoints_ascending_by_max_cfm.note: final/ceiling tier - also the unconditioned else clause in the formula
